% Auto-generated by src/compute_human_agreement.py
% Reproducible within-group pairwise agreement tables (A-D restricted).

\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Within-group pairwise agreement by gender (human respondents). Pairwise agreement is restricted to options A--D; see footnote~1 for the inclusion rule.}
\label{tab:human-gender-agree}
\small
\begin{tabular}{lrrrr}
\toprule
\textbf{Gender} & \textbf{N} & \textbf{Overall} & \textbf{Info Qs} & \textbf{Perception Qs} \\
\midrule
Male & 35 & 50.8\% & 53.7\% & 39.3\% \\
Female & 58 & 55.0\% & 59.5\% & 36.9\% \\
Non-binary$^*$ & 6 & 60.9\% & 65.0\% & 44.6\% \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\vspace{0.3em}
\begin{minipage}{0.95\linewidth}\scriptsize
Pairwise agreement = probability that two randomly chosen respondents in the subgroup selected the same response (A--D), averaged across questions; random baseline = 25\%. Male and Female agree on the plurality response for 35 of 40 questions (87.5\\%); disagreements occur on Q6 (perception: M\,=\,A, F\,=\,B), Q14 (information: M\,=\,D, F\,=\,C), Q28 (perception: M\,=\,A, F\,=\,B), Q37 (perception: M\,=\,B, F\,=\,D), Q39 (perception: M\,=\,B, F\,=\,A).\\
$^*$ N=6; estimates unreliable for this group.
\end{minipage}
\end{table}

\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Within-group pairwise agreement by education level (human respondents). Pairwise agreement is restricted to options A--D; see footnote~1 for the inclusion rule.}
\label{tab:human-edu-agree}
\small
\begin{tabular}{lrrrr}
\toprule
\textbf{Education} & \textbf{N} & \textbf{Overall} & \textbf{Info Qs} & \textbf{Perception Qs} \\
\midrule
Low & 49 & 54.8\% & 58.9\% & 38.2\% \\
High & 50 & 52.3\% & 56.0\% & 37.2\% \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\vspace{0.3em}
\begin{minipage}{0.95\linewidth}\scriptsize
Pairwise agreement = probability that two randomly chosen respondents in the subgroup selected the same response (A--D), averaged across questions; random baseline = 25\%. Low- and high-education groups agree on the plurality response for 33 of 40 questions (82.5\\%); disagreements occur on Q6 (perception: Low\,=\,A, High\,=\,B), Q14 (information: Low\,=\,C, High\,=\,D), Q24 (information: Low\,=\,D, High\,=\,B), Q28 (perception: Low\,=\,A, High\,=\,B), Q37 (perception: Low\,=\,B, High\,=\,D), Q45 (information: Low\,=\,D, High\,=\,A), Q48 (perception: Low\,=\,B, High\,=\,D). 0 questions excluded because neither education stratum produced a uniquely-defined plurality on those questions.
\end{minipage}
\end{table}
